test.command:
cd /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension && python3 - <<'PY'
from pathlib import Path
import os, subprocess, json
root = Path('.')
mid = 'scrutinyfoundationtmp'
mission_dir = Path.home()/'.gemini-mc'/'missions'/f'mis_{mid}'
if mission_dir.exists():
    subprocess.run(['rm','-rf', str(mission_dir)], check=True)
subprocess.run(['./scripts/scaffold-mission.sh', mid, str(root.resolve())], check=True)
subprocess.run(['./scripts/validate-state.sh', str(mission_dir/'state.json')], check=True)
print(json.dumps({'mission_dir': str(mission_dir), 'state_exists': (mission_dir/'state.json').exists(), 'features_exists': (mission_dir/'features.json').exists(), 'messages_exists': (mission_dir/'messages.jsonl').exists()}))
PY

lint.command: cd /Users/codex/Projects/gemini-mission-control-extension && jq . gemini-extension.json && for f in scripts/*.sh; do test -x "$f" && bash -n "$f" && echo "$f"; done && gemini extensions link .

blockingIssues.description: scripts/validate-state.sh only checks that createdAt and updatedAt are strings, so invalid non-ISO-8601 timestamps still pass validation.
scripts/scaffold-mission.sh interpolates workingDirectory into JSON without escaping, which can produce invalid state.json for quoted, backslash-containing, or multiline paths.
commands/mission-status.toml falls back to directory mtime when selecting the most recent mission, so it can report the wrong mission instead of using state.json updatedAt as required.

appliedUpdates.description: Updated .factory/library/environment.md to state that shellcheck is not installed and bash -n is the supported shell validation command in this environment.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Document that feature skillName values may correspond to project-local skills under .factory/skills/, and tell workers exactly where to look before concluding a skill is unavailable.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Reviews for extension-manifest-and-structure, gemini-md-context, scaffold-script-and-state-schema, basic-commands, and remaining-commands-toml all observed workers claiming extension-builder was unavailable even though .factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md exists.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Update worker/build skills so shell script verification explicitly uses bash -n in this repo instead of attempting shellcheck discovery.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The hooks-and-agents-format review found the worker attempted a shellcheck path even though mission guidance says shellcheck is not installed; this caused unnecessary divergence from the repo's supported verification workflow.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Clarify that handoffs and review summaries must record exact commands that succeeded, including any uninstall/retry steps, rather than simplified equivalents.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: The extension-manifest-and-structure and remaining-commands-toml reviews found inaccurate handoff claims about skill availability and the exact gemini extensions link sequence, reducing confidence in execution records.

rejectedObservations.observation: services.yaml only exposes primitive validators and no single project-level validation command
rejectedObservations.reason: rejected as ambiguous; the current additive validator commands are factually correct for this declarative repo and the observation does not justify a safe automated change without orchestrator direction.

rejectedObservations.observation: Repeated reports that extension-builder was unavailable despite existing in .factory/skills/extension-builder/SKILL.md
rejectedObservations.reason: deduplicated into a single systemic guidance recommendation rather than repeating it as multiple separate findings.
